factors.summary: Market share is stable or improving, particularly in segments where demand is strong.

evidence.text: The company is gaining or maintaining market share in key segments as demand increases.

factors.summary: Competitive dynamics are stable, with the company focusing on differentiation through technology and innovation.

evidence.text: The New Power segment [...] is currently in the development phase with a primary focus on research and development activities for our power systems, components and subsystems.

factors.summary: Order backlog is growing, with new orders outpacing revenue, particularly in the Components segment.

evidence.text: Components segment sales increased 43 percent largely due to higher emission solutions demand in China, India and North America.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting toward higher-margin segments, particularly in the Components and Power Systems segments, driven by increased demand in emission solutions and power generation.

evidence.text: Components segment sales increased 43 percent largely due to higher emission solutions demand in China, India and North America. Power Systems segment sales increased 16 percent primarily due to higher demand in power generation markets in North America, China, Middle East and Asia Pacific.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is accelerating, with all operating segments contributing to the increase.

evidence.text: Worldwide revenues increased 22 percent in the three months ended April 4, 2021, compared to the same period in 2020, due to higher demand in all operating segments and most geographic regions of the world.

factors.summary: Cost reduction initiatives are partially offsetting cost increases, with selling, general and administrative expenses decreasing as a percentage of sales.

evidence.text: Selling, general and administrative expenses as a percentage of sales decreased to 9.4 percent in the three months ended April 4, 2021, from 10.9 percent in the comparable period in 2020. The decrease in selling, general and administrative expenses as a percentage of sales was mainly due to sales increasing faster than selling, general and administrative expenses, despite higher variable compensation expenses.

factors.summary: Productivity is improving, as evidenced by higher gross margins and operational efficiency gains.

evidence.text: The increase in gross margin was primarily due to higher volumes and improved pricing, partially offset by increased compensation expenses (especially variable compensation) and higher premium freight costs.

factors.summary: Capacity utilization is increasing, driven by higher demand across segments.

evidence.text: Components segment sales increased 43 percent largely due to higher emission solutions demand in China, India and North America. Engine segment sales increased 14 percent due to increased volumes in the North American pick-up truck and heavy-duty truck markets.

factors.summary: Product quality and safety are stable, with no significant incidents or recalls mentioned.

evidence.text: Our products are used in trucks of all sizes, buses and recreational vehicles, as well as in various industrial applications, including construction, agriculture, power generation systems and other off-highway applications.

factors.summary: Regulatory changes, particularly in emissions standards, are creating costs and operational constraints, but the company is managing through them.

evidence.text: Future bans or limitations on the use of diesel-powered products; increasingly stringent environmental laws and regulations; [...] Research activities continue to focus on development of new products to meet future emission standards around the world.

factors.summary: Environmental regulations and emissions requirements are creating significant compliance costs and operational constraints, particularly in meeting future standards.

evidence.text: Research activities continue to focus on development of new products to meet future emission standards around the world, improvements in fuel economy performance of diesel and natural gas powered engines and related components as well as development activities around fully electric, hybrid and hydrogen powertrain solutions.

factors.summary: Trade tariffs and policy changes are creating risks, particularly related to international trade and the U.K.'s exit from the European Union.

evidence.text: Policy changes in international trade; the U.K.'s exit from the European Union.

factors.summary: Litigation exposure is present, but specifics are not detailed in the provided text.

evidence.text: The outcome of pending and future litigation and governmental proceedings.

factors.summary: Management has not explicitly raised or lowered forward guidance in the provided text.

evidence.text: As of the date of this filing, our credit ratings and outlooks from the credit rating agencies remain unchanged.

factors.summary: Management appears confident, as evidenced by continued investment in strategic initiatives and maintaining credit ratings.

evidence.text: As of the date of this filing, our credit ratings and outlooks from the credit rating agencies remain unchanged.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include supply chain disruptions, labor relations, and geopolitical factors, among others.

evidence.text: Readers are cautioned not to place undue reliance on forward-looking statements, which speak only as of the date hereof. Future factors that could affect the outcome of forward-looking statements include the following: [...] disruptions in global credit and financial markets as the result of the COVID-19 pandemic; labor relations or work stoppages; reliance on our executive leadership team and other key personnel; climate change and global warming; [...] political, economic and other risks from operations in numerous countries; competitor activity; increasing competition, including increased global competition among our customers in emerging markets; foreign currency exchange rate changes; [...] other risk factors described in our 2020 Form 10-K, Part I, Item 1A. under the caption 'Risk Factors.'

factors.summary: Long-term strategic direction is clear, focusing on electrification and alternative power technologies, with investments in new product development.

evidence.text: We continue to serve all our markets as they adopt electrification and alternative power technologies, meeting the needs of our OEM partners and end customers. [...] Research activities continue to focus on development of new products to meet future emission standards around the world, improvements in fuel economy performance of diesel and natural gas powered engines and related components as well as development activities around fully electric, hybrid and hydrogen powertrain solutions.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include foreign currency exchange rates, commodity prices, and geopolitical risks, which could impact near-term results.

evidence.text: Foreign currency exchange rate changes; the performance of our pension plan assets and volatility of discount rates, particularly those related to the sustained slowdown of the global economy due to the COVID-19 pandemic; the price and availability of energy; the outcome of pending and future litigation and governmental proceedings; continued availability of financing, financial instruments and financial resources in the amounts, at the times and on the terms required to support our future business; and other risk factors described in our 2020 Form 10-K, Part I, Item 1A. under the caption 'Risk Factors.'

factors.summary: New product launches and investments in electrification and alternative power solutions are catalysts for future growth.

evidence.text: Research activities continue to focus on development of new products to meet future emission standards around the world, improvements in fuel economy performance of diesel and natural gas powered engines and related components as well as development activities around fully electric, hybrid and hydrogen powertrain solutions.

factors.summary: No specific mention of headcount restructuring or workforce changes is made in the provided text.

evidence.text: We serve our customers through a network of over 500 wholly-owned, joint venture and independent distributor locations and over 9,000 Cummins certified dealer locations with service to approximately 190 countries and territories.

factors.summary: Labor relations are stable, with no mention of active or potential strikes or contract disputes.

evidence.text: We are a global power leader that designs, manufactures, distributes and services diesel, natural gas, electric and hybrid powertrains and powertrain-related components including filtration, aftertreatment, turbochargers, fuel systems, controls systems, air handling systems, automated transmissions, electric power generation systems, batteries, electrified power systems, hydrogen production and fuel cell products.

evidence.text: Our financial performance depends, in large part, on varying conditions in the markets we serve, particularly the on-highway, construction and general industrial markets.

evidence.text: Our financial performance depends, in large part, on varying conditions in the markets we serve, particularly the on-highway, construction and general industrial markets.

[...] The New Power segment designs, manufactures, sells and supports hydrogen production solutions as well as electrified power systems ranging from fully electric to hybrid along with innovative components and subsystems, including battery and fuel cell technologies.

factors.summary: Equipment replacement cycles are accelerating, particularly in North American on-highway markets, contributing to higher sales.

evidence.text: Net sales in the U.S. and Canada improved 7 percent, primarily due to increased demand in North American on-highway markets, which also positively impacted all of our components businesses, partially offset by reduced sales in our distribution product lines resulting from supply chain constraints.

factors.summary: Dealer/distributor inventory levels are healthy, with orders signaling growing end-user demand.

evidence.text: We serve our customers through a network of over 500 wholly-owned, joint venture and independent distributor locations and over 9,000 Cummins certified dealer locations with service to approximately 190 countries and territories.

factors.summary: The company's debt leverage is stable with a slight improvement in the debt to capital ratio, indicating a positive trend in reducing financial leverage. The balance sheet is strengthening through deleveraging, supported by strong liquidity and access to credit markets.

evidence.text: Our financial condition and liquidity remain strong. Our solid balance sheet and credit ratings enable us to have ready access to credit and the capital markets.

Our debt to capital ratio (total capital defined as debt plus equity) at April 4, 2021, was 31.2 percent, compared to 31.7 percent at December 31, 2020. The decrease was primarily due to $73 million of lower debt balances since December 31, 2020.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is stable to strong, with $339 million generated in the first quarter of 2021, slightly down from $379 million in the prior year period. The company benefits from strong cash from operations and has available credit facilities supporting liquidity.

evidence.text: Cash provided by operations is typically our principal source of liquidity with $339 million generated in the three months ended April 4, 2021. Our sources of liquidity include: ... Available credit capacity Revolving credit facilities (2) $ 3,183 ...
We generated $339 million of cash from operations for the three months ended April 4, 2021, compared to $379 million for the comparable period in 2020.

factors.summary: The company is actively enhancing shareholder value through both stock repurchases and dividend payments. In the first three months of 2021, they spent $418 million on repurchases and paid $197 million in dividends, indicating a strong commitment to returning value to shareholders. However, there is a discrepancy in the dividend payment figures, with the first subsection mentioning $197 million and the second not specifying dividend payments during this period, which could suggest varying priorities or focus areas over time. Overall, the company's actions demonstrate a positive approach to maintaining and increasing shareholder returns.

evidence.text: We intend to repurchase outstanding shares from time to time during 2021 to enhance shareholder value. We paid dividends of $197 million during the three months ended April 4, 2021.
In the first three months of 2021, we purchased $418 million, or 1.7 million shares of common stock.

factors.summary: The company has a mixed capital expenditure outlook, with a clear plan to increase spending in North America while also investing in research and development for new power solutions. While specific figures are provided for North America investments, the New Power segment's focus on R&D suggests a growth-oriented approach, though without explicit details on future CapEx trajectories beyond 2021.

evidence.text: We plan to spend an estimated $725 million to $775 million in 2021 on capital expenditures, excluding internal use software, with over 50 percent of these expenditures expected to be invested in North America.
The New Power segment [...] is currently in the development phase with a primary focus on research and development activities for our power systems, components and subsystems.

Research activities continue to focus on development of new products to meet future emission standards around the world, improvements in fuel economy performance of diesel and natural gas powered engines and related components as well as development activities around fully electric, hybrid and hydrogen powertrain solutions.

Our effective tax rate for 2021 is expected to approximate 22.5 percent, excluding any discrete items that may arise.

Political, economic and other risks from operations in numerous countries.

evidence.text: Our financial condition and liquidity remain strong. Our solid balance sheet and credit ratings enable us to have ready access to credit and the capital markets.

Worldwide revenues increased 22 percent in the three months ended April 4, 2021, compared to the same period in 2020, due to higher demand in all operating segments and most geographic regions of the world.

evidence.text: Unit shipments by engine classification [...] were as follows: [...] Total unit shipments 172,300 [...] 26 %

Engine segment sales increased 14 percent due to increased volumes in the North American pick-up truck and heavy-duty truck markets.

factors.summary: The company's MA activity is primarily focused on investments in joint ventures and other entities, particularly in emerging markets like China and India, which have contributed to growth. However, the company is currently inactive in acquisitions and mergers.

evidence.text: No specific information on acquisitions or mergers is provided in the text.

Equity, royalty and interest income from investees increased $37 million for the three months ended April 4, 2021, versus the comparable period in 2020, primarily due to increased earnings at Dongfeng Cummins Engine Co., Ltd., Beijing Foton Cummins Engine Co., Ltd., Tata Cummins Ltd. (excluding the absence of 2020 benefits noted below), Guangxi Cummins Industrial Power Co., Loop Energy Inc. and Dongfeng Cummins Emission Solutions Co., Ltd., partially offset by the absence of a $37 million favorable adjustment as the result of tax changes within India's 2020-2021 Union Budget of India (India Tax Law Changes) passed in March 2020 and $18 million of technology fee revenue both recorded in the first quarter of 2020 in Tata Cummins Ltd.
evidence.subsection: Equity, Royalty and Interest Income from Investees

factors.summary: Demand trends are positive across multiple regions, including North America, China, and India, with strong expectations for pick-up trucks and aftermarket business. Overall demand is accelerating, supported by higher sales across all operating segments and geographic regions, leading to a 22% increase in worldwide revenues in the first quarter of 2021 compared to the same period in 2020.

evidence.text: We expect demand for pick-up trucks in North America to remain strong. [...] We anticipate our aftermarket business will continue to improve, driven primarily by increased truck utilization in North America.

Worldwide revenues increased 22 percent in the three months ended April 4, 2021, compared to the same period in 2020, due to higher demand in all operating segments and most geographic regions of the world.

factors.summary: The end-market momentum is positive, with construction and general industrial markets growing fastest, particularly in China. Additionally, on-highway markets are contributing to growth.

evidence.text: Components segment sales for the three months ended April 4, 2021, increased $650 million [...] Turbo technologies sales increased $97 million [...]

Higher demand in all components businesses (primarily in China and India), stronger demand in China due to an improved COVID-19 environment over the comparable period in 2020, higher off-highway demand (mainly construction markets in China), higher demand for power generation equipment and favorable foreign currency impacts of 3 percent of international sales (primarily the Chinese renminbi and Euro, partially offset by the Brazilian real).

factors.summary: Labor costs are increasing, particularly in variable compensation, which is contributing to margin pressure. Despite this increase, the efficiency of labor costs has improved due to higher sales growth outpacing the rise in expenses.

evidence.text: The increase in selling, general and administrative expenses was due to increased variable compensation expenses [...]
Selling, general and administrative expenses increased $28 million [...] primarily due to increased variable compensation expenses [...].

factors.summary: The company is exhibiting mixed evidence regarding pricing power. While there is some indication of increased gross margins due to higher volumes and favorable mix, there are also signs that higher compensation and freight costs are partially offsetting these gains.

evidence.text: The increase in gross margin and gross margin as a percentage of sales was mainly due to higher volumes and favorable mix, partially offset by increased compensation expenses.

Gross margin increased $192 million for the three months ended April 4, 2021 and decreased 1.4 points as a percentage of sales, versus the comparable period in 2020. The increase in gross margin was primarily due to higher volumes and improved pricing, partially offset by increased compensation expenses (especially variable compensation) and higher premium freight costs.

factors.summary: Input costs are rising, primarily driven by increased premium freight and unfavorable foreign currency fluctuations. While there have been some improvements in gross margin due to higher volumes and pricing, these gains are being offset by higher compensation expenses, particularly variable compensation, and ongoing issues with premium freight costs and manufacturing inefficiencies.

evidence.text: The decrease in gross margin and gross margin as a percentage of sales was mainly due to increased premium freight, unfavorable mix, unfavorable foreign currency fluctuations [...]

The increase in gross margin was primarily due to higher volumes and improved pricing, partially offset by increased compensation expenses (especially variable compensation) and higher premium freight costs. The decrease in gross margin as a percentage of sales was primarily due to higher compensation expenses, increased premium freight costs and manufacturing inefficiencies.

factors.summary: The company's margins show a mixed trend. While gross margins have increased in absolute terms, there has been a slight compression in the margin percentage. Operating margins, however, have shown significant improvement, expanding by 28 percent year-over-year.

evidence.text: Gross margin increased $192 million for the three months ended April 4, 2021 and decreased 1.4 points as a percentage of sales, versus the comparable period in 2020.
Operating income increased $176 million, or 28 percent, to $810 million from $634 million.
Segment EBITDA as a percentage of total sales [...] 19.6 % [...] 18.6 % [...] 1.0

factors.summary: The aftermarket service revenue is growing, driven by increased truck utilization in North America and the company's installed base and recurring revenue opportunities. However, the business is also exposed to market conditions in various industries, which could impact its financial performance.

evidence.text: We anticipate our aftermarket business will continue to improve, driven primarily by increased truck utilization in North America.

Our financial performance depends, in large part, on varying conditions in the markets we serve, particularly the on-highway, construction and general industrial markets. [...] At the same time, our geographic diversity and broad product and service offerings have helped limit the impact from a drop in demand in any one industry or customer or the economy of any single country on our consolidated results.

factors.summary: Construction activity and infrastructure spending are driving demand, particularly in China and India. The evidence highlights strong demand across various product lines and regions, with notable growth in China due to improved COVID-19 conditions and increased spending in construction and power generation sectors.

evidence.text: Components segment sales [...] Turbo technologies sales increased $97 million [...] primarily due to increased demand in China, Western Europe and North America.

Higher demand in all components businesses (primarily in China and India), stronger demand in China due to an improved COVID-19 environment over the comparable period in 2020, higher off-highway demand (mainly construction markets in China), higher demand for power generation equipment and favorable foreign currency impacts of 3 percent of international sales (primarily the Chinese renminbi and Euro, partially offset by the Brazilian real).

factors.summary: Foreign exchange movements are helping, particularly in the Chinese renminbi and Euro, contributing to favorable translation effects.

evidence.text: The foreign currency translation adjustment was a net loss of $56 million [...] driven by the following: [...] Brazilian real, British pound, Chinese renminbi.
evidence.subsection: Comprehensive Income - Foreign Currency Translation Adjustment

evidence.text: Favorable foreign currency fluctuations of 1 percent of total sales, primarily in the Chinese renminbi and Euro, partially offset by the Brazilian real.

factors.summary: The company is increasing its R&D spending, particularly in the New Power segment and other areas, to support growth. The investment is focused on new product development for electrification and alternative power solutions, including fully electric, hybrid, and hydrogen powertrain solutions.

evidence.text: Research, development and engineering expenses [...] increased [...] due to higher compensation expenses, especially variable compensation expenses.

Research activities continue to focus on development of new products to meet future emission standards around the world, improvements in fuel economy performance of diesel and natural gas powered engines and related components as well as development activities around fully electric, hybrid and hydrogen powertrain solutions.

factors.summary: The supply chain health is facing significant challenges, primarily due to supply constraints and disruptions, including semiconductor shortages, which have led to increased costs, manufacturing delays, and potential loss of sales. Additionally, premium freight costs and manufacturing inefficiencies are contributing to ongoing disruptions.

evidence.text: Supply constraints driven by strong demand in multiple end markets and regions may lead to increased costs, including higher premium freight.
The shortage of key components such as semiconductor chips may lead to manufacturing delays, increased costs and the loss of sales.
The decrease in gross margin as a percentage of sales was primarily due to higher compensation expenses, increased premium freight costs and manufacturing inefficiencies.

factors.summary: The company is experiencing increasing inventory levels, driven by higher demand and supply chain constraints. Despite efforts to manage inventory, challenges in the supply chain may impact future levels.

evidence.text: Inventories $ 3,753 [...] compared to $ 3,425 [...]
